steps.text: Compute the means. Data set A: nine 6s. Mean$_A = \frac{9\times6}{9} = 6$. Data set B: nine 6s plus one 90. Mean$_B = \frac{9\times6+90}{10} = \frac{144}{10} = 14.4$. The means are different.

Compute the medians. Data set A has 9 values; the median is the 5th value when sorted: all are 6, so Median$_A = 6$. Data set B has 10 values sorted as 6,6,6,6,6,6,6,6,6,90; the median is the average of the 5th and 6th values: $\frac{6+6}{2}=6$. So Median$_B = 6$. The medians are the same.

Evaluate each choice:
  • Choice A — Only the means are different.: the means do differ (6 vs 14.4) and the medians are both 6. That confirms A is correct.
  • Choice B — Only the medians are different.: the medians are both 6 (not different), and the means are different. Incorrect.
  • Choice C — Both the means and the medians are different.: the medians are equal, so this is false. Incorrect.
  • Choice D — Neither the means nor the medians are different.: the means do differ, so this is false. Incorrect.
